title: Riders enjoy local show
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE NEWS - Local horse riders participated in a practice showing show held at Déjà Vu Equestrian and Cottages on Saturday 13 April.

Everybody had a delightful time as riders showcased their magnificent horses and ponies while also honing their skills.


> Showing, an equestrian discipline aimed at exhibiting perfect examples of specific horse breeds, fosters meticulous attention to detail and a sense of pride in presentation.


It serves as a solid foundation in horsemanship, sportsmanship, and a lifelong love for horses, catering to participants of all ages.

PHOTO GALLERY: Equestrian show at Déjà Vu Equestrian and Cottages

Show riding horses and ponies are evaluated on their beauty, training, impeccable manners and gracefulness.

On the other hand, Working riding horses and ponies undergo various tests, including navigating bending poles, to assess their obedience, disposition and temperament.

Emily Anderson with Jesse. Photo: Esme Spicer

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
